notes_md:
**chỉnh** = to adjust, tweak, or fix something so it’s correct or better. It’s used for small changes, not big repairs.

You use it with things you can fine-tune: settings, clothes, documents, photos, machines, posture, tone, etc. Very often combined with **lại** to mean “adjust again” or “fix back to normal.”


It can also mean “edit” when talking about text, videos, or images. Close synonym: **sửa** (fix) — but *sửa* is broader, while *chỉnh* feels more like fine-tuning.

translation.en: I need to adjust the painting.
I want to adjust my computer.

content.sentence: Tôi muốn ___ máy tính của mình.

exampleSentences: Anh có thể chỉnh âm lượng nhỏ lại một chút được không?
Em phải chỉnh lại đồng hồ báo thức cho đúng giờ.
Chị thường chỉnh tư thế ngồi khi làm việc lâu trên máy tính.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you adjust the volume a bit lower?
I have to adjust the alarm clock to the correct time.
She often adjusts her sitting posture when working long hours on the computer.
notes:
chỉnh = to adjust, tweak, or fix something so it’s correct or better. It’s used for small changes, not big repairs.

You use it with things you can fine-tune: settings, clothes, documents, photos, machines, posture, tone, etc. Very often combined with lại to mean “adjust again” or “fix back to normal.”

It can also mean “edit” when talking about text, videos, or images. Close synonym: sửa (fix) — but sửa is broader, while chỉnh feels more like fine-tuning.

example.vi: Tôi cần chỉnh lại bài báo này.
example.en: I need to edit this article.

definitions.es: Ajustar o editar algo para mejorarlo.
definitions.en: To adjust or edit something to improve it.
definitions.vi: Điều chỉnh hoặc chỉnh sửa một cái gì đó để cải thiện.
